title: lichen_frame_write indexes addr_lens before validating addr_mode
description: Trigger: a caller passes a struct lichen_frame with frame->addr_mode outside the defined address-mode range, e.g. 4, to lichen_frame_write(). The function only rejects LICHEN_ADDR_ELIDED at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/frame.c:117, then indexes addr_lens[frame->addr_mode] at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/frame.c:130. Because addr_lens has four entries (0..3) at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/frame.c:22, invalid values read past the table before any range check. Impact: out-of-bounds read in frame serialization, potentially selecting a bogus address length and causing subsequent memcpy/frame length calculations to operate on an unintended size. Fix: validate frame->addr_mode is within ARRAY_SIZE(addr_lens) before indexing, and reject unsupported modes explicitly.
